title: DASH-4: End-to-end verification of Phase 1
description:
## Goal

Prove the pipeline works before pointing real workers at it.

## Scope

- Boot server + client
- Send synthetic event sequences simulating 2–3 workers across different orgs/projects:
  - normal working flow (SessionStart → tools → Stop)
  - blocked flow (Notification permission_prompt → needs-you flag)
  - resume flow (same worktree, new session_id → same card)
- Verify board renders cards, statuses transition, drill-down filters correctly
- Verify real hooks from one actual Claude Code session in a test worktree

## Done when

All flows above pass; README updated with setup steps for new worktrees.

title: DASH-3: Client: Board view homepage
description:
## Goal

Flip the primary view from event timeline to a per-worker board — the "at a glance" answer to "what's going on and who needs me."

## Scope

- New default homepage: one card per worker, grouped org → project
- Card contents: title (fallback: repo + branch), lifecycle badge, "⚠️ needs you" flag, ticket label, last activity (relative), model name
- `useWorkers.ts` composable: initial fetch from `GET /workers` + live updates from WS
- Existing timeline demoted to drill-down: click a card → timeline filtered to that worker
- View toggle in header (Board / Timeline)
- Stale workers (no events 30+ min) visually muted

## Done when

- Multiple concurrent workers render as separate cards with live status
- Needs-you flag appears on Notification/PermissionRequest and clears when work resumes
- Card click opens the filtered timeline

title: DASH-2: Server: schema, workers table, lifecycle status
description:
## Goal

Server understands workers as first-class entities and derives lifecycle status from the event stream.

## Scope

- Extend `events` table with worker fields (additive migration, same pattern as existing column checks in `db.ts`)
- New `workers` table: worker_id (PK), org, project, host, branch, ticket, title, cwd, last session_id, last_event_type, last_activity, status, needs_attention
- Upsert worker row on every incoming event
- Lifecycle mapping:
  - SessionStart / UserPromptSubmit / PreToolUse / PostToolUse / Subagent* / PreCompact → `working`
  - Stop → `idle` (finished responding, may be waiting on review)
  - Notification / PermissionRequest → `blocked` + needs_attention flag
  - SessionEnd → `ended`
  - needs_attention clears on next UserPromptSubmit or tool use
- `GET /workers` endpoint
- Broadcast worker upserts over existing WebSocket (`{type: 'worker', data}`); include workers in the initial WS payload

## Done when

- Posting a synthetic event sequence produces correct worker rows and status transitions
- WS clients receive worker updates in real time

## Open question

Stale rule: gray out workers with no events for 30+ min (SessionEnd doesn't always fire). Decide threshold during DASH-3.

title: DASH-1: Worker identity layer in hooks
description:
## Goal

Every hook event carries a stable worker identity so the dashboard can key cards on workers, not ephemeral sessions.

## Scope

- `identity.py` in `.claude/hooks/utils/`: resolves worker_id, org, project, host, branch, ticket, cwd
- Stable `worker_id`: UUID written to `<worktree>/.claude/worker-id` on first event (survives resumes; `session_id` is ephemeral)
- Hybrid derivation: org = git remote owner, project = repo name, ticket = branch regex (`ABC-123`)
- `.claude/dashboard.json` overrides any derived field: `{"org", "project", "ticket", "title"}`
- `send_event.py` attaches the result as a `worker` object on every event
- Fail-safe: identity errors must never block event delivery

## Done when

- Events posted from any git worktree include a populated `worker` object
- Same worker_id across session resumes in the same worktree

## Notes

Prototyped in scratch; derivation validated against the fork's own repo (org `wsaults` from remote).

title: DASH-9: Linear + GitHub enrichment on cards
description: Pull live Linear ticket state and GitHub PR review status via API/MCP, keyed off the ticket ref and branch already on each worker card. Server-side polling with cache; card shows ticket status + PR checks/review state.

title: DASH-8: Centralize: agents dial out over Tailscale
description: Split agent ↔ central server. Agents on each machine dial out over a persistent WebSocket (Tailscale network), so no inbound ports on worker machines. Dashboard groups by org → project → host; control commands route back down the agent's WS.

title: DASH-7: tmux control layer (opt-in per worker)
description: Opt-in: launch a worker in a named detached tmux session to get dashboard actions (answer prompt, nudge) via `tmux send-keys`. Everyday Warp-tab workers stay read-only — Warp and tmux don't mix well, so control is reserved for workers you explicitly want to drive remotely.

title: DASH-6: Semantic status self-report (status.json)
description: Workers self-report `title` and `next_steps` into `<worktree>/.claude/status.json` — via a Stop-hook transcript summary or a slash command. Identity layer already reads this file if present, so this ticket is about producing it reliably. "Questions" field cut per earlier decision.

title: DASH-5: Per-machine agent (forwarding + offline queue)
description: Thin local process: hooks POST to localhost agent → agent enriches (host context) and forwards upstream with an offline buffer. Upstream URL is config: localhost today, central server in Phase 4 with zero hook changes. Deferred from Phase 1 since it's near-passthrough on a single machine; its dial-out WebSocket is what later carries control commands back down (DASH-7/8).
